strengths: Strong Python + Pandas ETL and automation
weaknesses: Only 2+ years in direct sales analytics context
Limited explicit mention of scheduling jobs
No explicit experience with Google Sheets as a tool
improvement_bullets: Built Python automation for weekly sales and operations reports (ingesting POS and CRM data), including scheduling jobs and building reliable, repeatable pipelines for data ingestion and transformation, eliminating repetitive data entry, saving 8 hours per week, and improving reporting accuracy for faster strategic decisions.

Standardized organizational KPIs and data governance frameworks to ensure cross-functional alignment, resulting in a consistent "single source of truth" for all business stakeholders and a reduction in report discrepancies.

Analyzed a 250K+ row dataset using Pandas and NumPy to identify demographic clusters and geographic patterns, demonstrating segmentation-oriented analytical methods and performing data quality validation with Great Expectations to ensure pipeline reliability.
Optimized relational database queries (CTEs, window functions, joins) and aggregations to clean and reconcile testing data, improving data accuracy by 60%.

Translated complex technical findings into business recommendations during stakeholder meetings to close the gap between raw data and growth strategy, creating executive-level presentations and recurring business reviews.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Analytics Senior Analyst opportunity with Salesforce. I am interested in this opportunity because Salesforce's commitment to AI-driven customer success and workforce transformation deeply resonates with my passion for leveraging data to drive innovation and efficiency. What interests me about working for Salesforce is its approach to pioneering the agentic era excites inspires intrigues me because I believe this represents the future of business operations and customer engagement. I believe my strong Python and SQL skills and my proven ability in Tableau dashboard development would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a data-driven and analytical mindset. In fact, at Shine Masters Cleaning Services, I developed Power BI and Looker workflows to replace manual reporting, reducing data mismatches and improving work efficiency by 40%. This involved creating statistical models to identify high-value customer segments, which led to a 300% increase in revenue for the targeted segment over 12 months. Ultimately, I know that the Data Analytics Senior Analyst role at Salesforce would allow me to bring my Python and SQL expertise to driving scalable solutions to improve efficiency and productivity across the AMER Business Development organization. Additionally, my experience as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my stakeholder communication skills. I translated complex technical findings into business recommendations during stakeholder meetings, ensuring cross-functional alignment and a consistent "single source of truth" for all business stakeholders. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my Tableau dashboard building skills in working on optimizing technology usage within Salesforce to support outbound BDR motions at Salesforce. I know that my strong Python and SQL skills and my proven ability in Tableau dashboard development align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Analytics Senior Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
